revenue.filter: Consumer only: accountType = Private or (none). Excludes corporate.

new_vs_repeat.insight: Repeat consumer senders generate 90% of send volume. Consumer avg txn is ~$4,000 vs $27K for corporate. Retention drives consumer revenue.

unknown.note: Unknown platform — may include some API/partner traffic

frequency.note: Consumer transactions per user per month.

retention_brackets.insight: Retention improves at 30+ days — most senders are monthly, not weekly. The 7→14 day 25% drop-off is the 'one-time sender' cliff. Users who survive past 30 days become sticky (55%+ retention).

balance_health.summary: Balance product (US/CA/NZ) is growing fast. Track actual completions via Transaction Completed with transactionType filter, not client-side events. Currency Activation +178% over 12 weeks. NZ just launched and growing rapidly.
balance_health.note: Consumer only (accountType=Private or none). Balance is fully live in US and NZ only. CA shows partial activity (may be limited rollout).

transaction_completed_breakdown.note: Source: Transaction Completed filtered by transactionType + accountType=Private. This is the server-side source of truth.

weekly_trends_6mo.note: Balance product launched Dec 2025. Zero activity before that. Rapid growth since.

daily_volume_usd.note: From chart ba2fkaid on existing dashboard

registration_to_activation.description: Users who registered specifically for balance (source=banner/exchange/add funds)
registration_to_activation.note: NZ has highest conversion — new market enthusiasm

Currency account Home.note: CASING ISSUE: 'home' vs 'Home' — same entry point tracked as two values

issues.issue: CASING BUG CONFIRMED: Two separate events in Amplitude
issues.detail: 'Add Funds Summary Confirmed' (capital C): 1,232 events/month. 'Add Funds Summary confirmed' (lowercase c): 3,771 events/month. The funnel breaks because it only matches one casing.
issues.impact: Mobile Add Funds funnel shows <1% conversion but actual completion is 8% on iOS — the events are just split across two names.
issues.fix: Change xe-apollo analytics.const.ts line 460: 'Add Funds Summary confirmed' → 'Add Funds Summary Confirmed'

issues.detail: 'Currency account home' (728/week) vs 'Currency account Home' (414/week) — same page, two values.

currency_activation.insight: Massive growth in currency account adoption. Android has the highest activation rate (87%). Web is lowest (60%) — investigate if web activation flow has extra friction.

convert_balance.naming_issue: Consumer uses 'Convert Funds Accessed/Completed', Corporate uses 'Convert Balance Accessed/Completed' — DIFFERENT event names for similar actions

balance_usage.title: Balance Usage & Idle Money
balance_usage.fund_then_send_detail: Of 1,735 users who funded balance (90 days), only 289 (16.7%) used it to send within 30 days. 83% of funded balance sits idle.

US.note: 13.7K total activated users, but only 464 funded in Feb (9.9% activation-to-fund rate). Large inactive pool.

CA.note: Launched Feb 26. 1K users activated in 2 months.

NZ.note: Just launched Mar 26. 243 users activated in first partial month.

fraud_and_closures.title: Fraud & Account Closures Among Balance Users

US.note: 1,451 of 13,580 activated users (10.7%) had accounts CLOSED within 30 days. 875 (6.4%) were fraud-referred. This explains a big chunk of the 'inactive' pool — they were removed, not inactive.

CA.note: Much lower closure rate than US.

fraud_and_closures.insight: In US: 10.7% of balance users get closed + 6.4% fraud-referred = ~17% of activated users are fraud-related. Monthly US closures run ~1,300-1,500. The real 'available' user pool in US is ~13.6K activated minus ~1.5K closed = ~12.1K, of which ~464 fund (3.8% of available, not 9.9% of all activated). The fraud issue inflates activation numbers significantly.

user_growth_by_region.insight: US: 13.7K activated but ~1.5K were fraud-closed (10.7%). Real available pool ~12.1K, of which 464 fund monthly (3.8%). CA launched Feb, NZ launched Mar. Balance is fully live in US and NZ only.

unique_users_monthly_all_regions.insight: Send-via-Balance users grew from 71 (Dec) to 293 (Mar) — 4x in 3 months. Balance is becoming a real payment method.

frequency.note: Users top up ~1.5x/month and send ~1.4x/month. Low frequency — mostly one-off usage.

avg_amounts.insight: Top-up amounts declining ($4K→$1.9K) as more small users join. But send-via-balance amounts GROWING ($1.7K→$4.8K) — power users sending bigger amounts through balance.

balance_usage.idle_money_insight: $5.82M held in balances (49.2% utilization). Converts ($1.38M) are internal currency exchanges — money stays in the system. Real outflows are Send via Balance only. Over half of funded money sits idle. Users may be: (1) using balance for rate speculation (fund when rate is good, send later), (2) not understanding they can send from balance, (3) waiting for a specific rate. This is a major growth opportunity — prompt funded users to send.

balance_remaining_by_region.title: Money Held in Balances (Funded - Sent)
balance_remaining_by_region.period: Cumulative since balance launch (Dec 2025). Updated Mar 24.
balance_remaining_by_region.note: Consumer only (accountType != Corporate). Convert Balance is internal (USD→CAD etc.) — money stays in the system. Only Send via Balance is a true outflow. Regions by senderCountry.

NZ.note: NZ shows negative remaining — users sending more than funded in this window. Balance launched ~1 month ago. Users may have funded from a different senderCountry.

total.insight: $3.2M held in consumer balances (58.5% utilization). Converts ($0.8M) are internal currency exchanges — money stays in the system.

fund_balance_corporate.note: Corporate-only. Growing steadily. Uses separate FUND_BALANCE events.

send_money_web.label: Send Money (Web)
send_money_web.note: Quote Confirmed → Recipient Selected → Txn Summary Confirmed → Txn Created → Txn Completed. 5-day window. Consumer only (accountType != Corporate).

send_money_ios.label: Send Money (iOS)
send_money_ios.note: Same funnel steps. iOS has highest conversion because users are more committed at Quote Confirmed. Consumer only.

cases.note: Was 54k during Feb 16 spike. Now ~9k. Still high — 86% of SWW events have no location. Most are from mobile Error Popup handler that doesn't tag location.
cases.action: Fix: Add location property to RmtPopupComponent.analyticsErrorCallback in xe-apollo segment.ts

cases.note: Delta card network failures during send money. Consistent ~400/week.
Mastercard Debit card failures.
Open banking failures during send money.

Bank transfer instruction failures. Category contains the bank name (Bank of America, Barclays, HSBC, etc.). Indicates user couldn't complete wire transfer instructions for that specific receiving bank.
3DS card auth failures. Consistent ~1k/week. These overlap with Card Authorisation Failed event.
Mobile-only variant (note underscore vs camelCase). Doubled from 177 to 383 over 4 weeks. Investigate mobile send money errors.
cases.action: Check mobile app versions — this could be a regression in recent release.

cases.note: Registration flow errors.

error_popup.note: Every error popup shown to any mobile user. The error_title is what the user sees.

cases.case: Action timed out - please try it again later.
cases.note: 4,768 this partial week vs ~1,100 baseline. 4x SPIKE — something is timing out. Likely API latency or backend slowness.
cases.action: URGENT: Investigate what API calls are timing out. Check backend health, Segment delivery latency, payment processor response times.

cases.note: ~2k/week. Users left the app idle and their session expired. Normal mobile behavior.

cases.case: Failed to continue, please try again
cases.note: 578 this partial week vs ~300 baseline. Generic flow continuation failure.

cases.case: Failed to verify order - please try again
cases.note: 3x higher than 4 weeks ago (152→462). Order verification is failing more often.
cases.action: Investigate order verification endpoint. This correlates with the Transaction Failed spike.

cases.note: Biometric (TouchID/FaceID) enablement failures. High volume.
Generic catch-all error popup.
Recipient creation API failures.

cases.case: Failed to send again - please try again
cases.note: Repeat transfer failures.
2FA/verification code resend failures.

cases.case: Something went wrong with this location - please select another location
cases.note: Cash payout location errors.
Recipient validation failures.
Mobile wallet loading failures.
Account settings change failures (283 email + 214 password).
Apple Pay failures. Card_Transactions is the generic failure code from Apple Pay.
Top codes: (none)=bulk, PSAPI_007=declined (~180/week), PSAPI_015=insufficient funds (~45/week), PSAPI_006=expired/invalid.
Similar distribution as debit but lower volume.
Authorization declined by issuer. Declining trend — good.
Network errors connecting to payment processor. Low but watch for spikes.
Payment processor rejected transaction creation. ~300/week — this is the main driver of the spike. Was near 0 before Feb 2.
cases.action: Check Adyen/Cybersource integration changes around Feb 2.

cases.note: 3DS validation failing server-side. ~230/week.
Users locked out after too many failed 3DS attempts.
Compliance/fraud-restricted accounts trying to transact.
Stale card transaction being reused. Possible code bug.
Missing card type in request. LIKELY A CODE BUG — card type should always be set.
cases.action: Fix: check card type mapping in payment flow, ensure CardTypeId is always populated.

cases.note: Amount changed between quote and contract. Edge case where rate moved.
No error code set — generic failures or error object sent instead of code. 52% of all quote errors. Poor observability.
cases.action: Fix: ensure all quote error paths set errorCode property.

cases.note: User's amount below minimum. Expected — users testing small amounts.
Quote calculation service error. Dropping significantly from 6k to 2k. Improvement or less traffic?
Trading hours exceeded. Expected for out-of-hours users.
Users trying to send to unsupported corridors. Demand signal for new corridor expansion.
Recipient's payout method not available for this quote. Triggers Recipient Selection Failed.
Cybersource doesn't report card brand. US/Canada processor.

cases.case: Abandoned (user didn't complete in bank app)
cases.note: 63% of OB failures. User started but didn't complete in their bank app. Expected — not all users follow through.
Bank rejected the payment. Top banks: Deutsche Bank (369), ING Bank (186), BNP Paribas (128).

growth_opportunities.title: Mobile Error Recovery
growth_opportunities.description: Web recovers 41% of failed payments. Mobile only 10%.
growth_opportunities.action: Improve mobile retry UX: prominent 'Try Different Method' button, auto-suggest alternatives.

growth_opportunities.title: Biometric Consent Drop-off
growth_opportunities.description: 91% of users who start biometric verification on iOS abandon at the consent screen.
growth_opportunities.action: Redesign consent: explain why, show trust signals, simplify language.

growth_opportunities.title: Transaction Failed — PSP Errors Still Elevated
growth_opportunities.description: PSP create transaction (294/week) + PSP payer auth validation (234/week) = 528 failures/week that didn't exist before Feb 2.
growth_opportunities.action: Check Adyen/Cybersource integration changes around Feb 2. CardTypeId bug (23/week) is likely fixable in code.

growth_opportunities.title: Error Popup: 'Action timed out' SPIKING
growth_opportunities.description: Mobile users seeing 'Action timed out' errors at 4x the normal rate this week.
growth_opportunities.action: URGENT: Check API response times, backend health, network connectivity.

growth_opportunities.title: 'Failed to verify order' ELEVATED
growth_opportunities.description: Order verification failures tripled in 4 weeks. Correlates with Transaction Failed spike.
growth_opportunities.action: Investigate order verification endpoint — likely same root cause as PSP failures.

growth_opportunities.title: Quote Error Observability Gap
growth_opportunities.metric: 52% of quote errors have no errorCode
growth_opportunities.description: 18,243 quote errors/week with errorCode=(none). Impossible to diagnose.
growth_opportunities.action: Fix: ensure all code paths that fire Quote Error set the errorCode property.

growth_opportunities.title: SWW location=send_money RISING
growth_opportunities.description: Mobile send money errors (location=send_money with underscore) doubled. Different from web's location=sendMoney (camelCase).
growth_opportunities.action: Check recent mobile app releases for send money flow regressions.

growth_opportunities.title: Registration Funnel Leak
growth_opportunities.description: 86% of registration starters never become money transfer profiles.
growth_opportunities.action: Simplify Personal Details→Phone Number step (biggest drop).

growth_opportunities.title: India Corridor Optimization
growth_opportunities.description: India is 2-4x the next corridor. Any GBP/USD→INR improvement has outsized ROI.
growth_opportunities.action: Corridor-specific deep dive: quote error rates, payment method distribution, fee competitiveness.

growth_opportunities.title: QUOTE019 Corridor Demand Signal
growth_opportunities.description: Users trying to send to unsupported corridors. This is unmet demand.
growth_opportunities.action: Analyze which destination countries drive QUOTE019 errors — prioritize for corridor expansion.

top_corridors_note: Consumer only (accountType != Corporate). Ranked by revenueUSD. Volume column shows revenue in USD.
